  1. de.fhdwhannover.integrea.ga.core.selection

  3. Methodendetails
    1. select(Population)

Schnittstelle Selection<T>

Typparameter:
T - The gene type used in the population's chromosomes (e.g., Integer, Character).
Alle bekannten Implementierungsklassen:
LinearRankingSelection, RouletteSelection, StochasticUniversalSelection, TournamentSelection
public interface Selection<T>
Contract for selecting an individual from a population to act as a parent for the next generation.

Selection operators apply fitness-based pressure, heavily favoring stronger candidates while maintaining a probability for weaker candidates to survive in order to preserve population diversity.

  • Methodenübersicht

    Modifikator und Typ
    Methode
    Beschreibung
    Individual<T>
    select(Population<T> population)
    Executes a specific selection algorithm (e.g., Tournament, Roulette Wheel) to extract a single candidate.

  • Methodendetails

    • select

      Individual<T> select(Population<T> population)
      Executes a specific selection algorithm (e.g., Tournament, Roulette Wheel) to extract a single candidate.

      Constraint: The implementation must return a non-null Individual directly from the active Population. It should rely on the individual's unscaled fitness score and handle edge cases gracefully (like zero-fitness or identical-fitness populations) without crashing.

      Parameter:
      population - The current generation from which to select a parent.
      Gibt zurück:
      The chosen Individual.
